required: Videos and screenshots render inside a recognizable iPhone silhouette
All phone-framed media on the page share the same outer width and aspect pattern at each breakpoint
No layout regressions at 375px, 768px, and 1280px widths

items.title: Build reusable PhoneFrame component
items.description: Create src/components/PhoneFrame.tsx — a pure-CSS iPhone 16/17 Pro silhouette that wraps arbitrary children (video, image, or other node). Outer dark bezel (`bg-[#0A0A0A]` or `bg-black` with a subtle ring), outer rounded corners ≈ `rounded-[2.75rem]`, inner screen ≈ `rounded-[2.25rem]` `overflow-hidden bg-black`, and a small Dynamic Island pill absolutely positioned at the top center of the screen. Accept props: `children`, `ariaLabel?`, `className?` (for width control), `width?`/`height?` (intrinsic, default 886x1920 matching the hero video). Use CSS `aspectRatio` on the inner screen so it stays phone-shaped regardless of width. Keep markup semantic — the outer element is a `<figure>`.
acceptanceCriteria: PhoneFrame.tsx exports a default React functional component
Outer figure has dark bezel, rounded ~2.75rem, subtle border matching existing `border-[#1f1f1f]` palette, and a soft shadow
Inner screen uses aspectRatio derived from width/height props and clips children with overflow-hidden
A Dynamic Island pill (≈ 90x26 px at intrinsic 886-wide phone) is absolutely positioned at top center with rounded-full and a `#111111` or darker background
Component accepts `className` for outer sizing (e.g., `max-w-[300px]`) and forwards `ariaLabel` to the figure
No layout issues when children is a `
TypeScript clean (`npx tsc --noEmit`)

items.title: Rewire HeroVideo to use PhoneFrame
items.description: Replace the current `<figure>` wrapper in HeroVideo.tsx with `<PhoneFrame>`. The video becomes the child, rendered at `className='h-full w-full object-cover'`. Preserve all existing video attributes (autoPlay, muted, loop, playsInline, preload='metadata', poster, aria-label). Keep the component's public API (src, poster, ariaLabel, width, height, className).
acceptanceCriteria: HeroVideo.tsx imports PhoneFrame and uses it as the outer element
The video still autoplays muted with loop + playsInline + preload='metadata'
Poster frame displays before autoplay kicks in
Aria label is applied to the PhoneFrame figure
Public props (src, poster, ariaLabel, width, height, className) are unchanged
The component renders identically in the hero at 375 / 768 / 1280 px (tested via viewport resize in dev server or visual inspection)

items.title: Rewire StoryVideo to use PhoneFrame
items.description: Same treatment as FRAME-002 for StoryVideo.tsx. Replace the `<figure>` wrapper with `<PhoneFrame>`, keep all video attributes and the public API (src, poster, ariaLabel, width, height, maxWidthClass).
acceptanceCriteria: StoryVideo.tsx imports PhoneFrame and uses it as the outer element
maxWidthClass prop continues to control outer width (default `max-w-[300px] sm:max-w-[340px]`) and is forwarded as className to PhoneFrame
Autoplay/muted/loop/playsInline/poster/preload all preserved
Component renders identically in Tonight's Pour and Spotlight & Flights sections
Public props unchanged

items.title: Normalize screenshot widths across story sections
items.description: Currently the Share section uses `centered: true` + `max-w-[360px]` while Scan and Collection let screenshots fill the grid cell (~480-500px at desktop). Apply a shared cap so all screenshot pairs render at the same outer width and align with the StoryVideo widths. Remove the ad-hoc `centered` field on Share — instead, give every image-based story section the same grid + max-width treatment so all phones on the page match in size. Target: each screenshot renders at `max-w-[280px] sm:max-w-[320px]` (roughly matching StoryVideo caps) within a centered 2-column grid.
acceptanceCriteria: No story section visually renders at a different phone width than the others
`centered` field is either removed from the StorySection type or repurposed so all sections behave consistently
Screenshots are centered within their grid cells and the overall pair is centered in the page
Holds at 375 / 768 / 1280 px — on mobile, screenshots stack (1 col) and stay capped at the same max width; on desktop, 2-col grid with the pair centered

items.title: Wrap story-section screenshots in PhoneFrame
items.description: Replace the current `<figure>` + `aspect-[9/19]` + `object-contain` pattern for screenshots with `<PhoneFrame>`. The Image/ImageWithFallback becomes the child with `object-cover` so it fills the screen area. This is the big visual win — all six screenshots and both videos become iPhone silhouettes, giving the page a unified device-mockup aesthetic.
acceptanceCriteria: All six image-based screenshots render inside a PhoneFrame silhouette with visible bezel + Dynamic Island
The ImageWithFallback/Image inside the frame uses object-cover and fills the inner screen with no letterboxing
Alt text is preserved on the inner img
The outer PhoneFrame respects the same max-width cap used in FRAME-004 so videos and images match
No console errors or missing-key warnings in the story map

items.title: Verification: types, lint, visual check
items.description: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint`. Both must be clean (pre-existing warnings in opengraph-image.tsx, privacy/page.tsx, terms/page.tsx, ImageWithFallback.tsx are acceptable). Document any net-new warnings and fix them. Write a SUMMARY.md noting files added/modified, high-level change narrative, and recommended next steps (run build locally; visual QA at 375/768/1280).
acceptanceCriteria: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0
`npm run lint` has no new warnings or errors beyond the 4 pre-existing ones
SUMMARY.md written to the sprint folder
